hypothesis: If REINVENT, MolMIM, or Mol-AIR are pre-trained on large chemical corpora (ChEMBL/ZINC), fine-tuned on a curated CTF dataset, and subsequently optimized via reinforcement learning with expert-defined structural rewards, then they will generate a higher fraction of valid, unique, internally diverse, novel CTF candidates that pass expert filters (and achieve better FCD) than the same models trained without one or more of these transfer steps because combined knowledge transfer and reward shaping align the models’ priors with domain-specific constraints and objectives.
